claims.text: GDACS assessed an Orange alert for a forest fire in Fontainebleau, France covering the period 12-14 July 2026, citing approximately 2,280 hectares burned and about 814 people exposed in the fire-affected area, noting the fire "can have a medium humanitarian impact based on the burned area and the affected population and their vulnerability."

EU Copernicus Earth observation imagery confirmed a wildfire spreading through part of Fontainebleau Forest, Seine-et-Marne, France starting 12 July 2026, with more than 800 hectares burned as of 13 July 2026, prompting evacuations and disrupting transportation services in the region.

description: A wildfire broke out in Fontainebleau Forest, Seine-et-Marne, roughly 60km southeast of Paris, on 12 July 2026, spreading rapidly amid a European heatwave before being brought under control within about 48 hours. GDACS assessed an Orange alert for medium humanitarian impact, citing about 2,280 hectares burned and roughly 814 people exposed in the affected area; EU Copernicus imagery measured more than 800 hectares burned as of 13 July. The fire prompted evacuations and disrupted nearby road and rail links; no allowlisted authoritative source has reported any deaths.

impact.summary: GDACS's event report assessed an Orange alert (medium humanitarian impact) for a wildfire burning in Fontainebleau Forest, Seine-et-Marne, France between 12 and 14 July 2026, citing about 2,280 hectares burned and roughly 814 people exposed within the fire-affected area; EU Copernicus imagery separately measured more than 800 hectares burned as of 13 July. The fire prompted evacuations and disrupted nearby road and rail services before being brought under control within about 48 hours. No allowlisted authoritative source has reported any deaths or a confirmed evacuation count.

location.region: Fontainebleau Forest, Seine-et-Marne (~60km SE of Paris)

name: Wildfire — Fontainebleau Forest, France
